answers.answer: ExtensionActivationTimes class tracks timing metrics during extension activation, capturing three measurements: codeLoadingTime (time to load extension code), activateCallTime (time for activate() call to return), and activateResolvedTime (time for activation promise to resolve)

FailedExtension (or MissingExtensionDependencyError class) represents an extension whose dependency failed. It passes null for exports, false for activationFailed, and a MissingExtensionDependency error to its parent ActivatedExtension constructor
The environmentVariableCollection property provides access to environment variable collection management. It is backed by IExtHostTerminalService (specifically the ExtHostTerminalService)
The three valid values for visibility property are: 'visible', 'collapsed', and 'hidden'. The default value is 'visible'

_alreadyActivatedEvents is a Map<string, boolean> that tracks which activation events have already been processed to prevent duplicate activations. activateByEvent checks this map before activating and adds events to prevent re-triggering

MainContext defines approximately 60-70 proxy identifiers. Three notebook-related identifiers are: MainThreadNotebook, MainThreadNotebookKernels, and MainThreadNotebookDocuments (or MainThreadNotebookEditors)
The _activateAllStartupFinishedDeferred method uses a time budget of 50 milliseconds when deferring activation of onStartupFinished extensions
The two special background color IDs are 'statusBarItem.warningBackground' and 'statusBarItem.errorBackground', which map to StatusbarEntryKind.Warning and StatusbarEntryKind.Error respectively
After registering all ExtHost proxy identifiers, the assertRegistered() method is called on rpcProtocol to verify that all expected identifiers have been properly registered
The three ExtensionHostKind values are: LocalProcess, LocalWebWorker, and Remote (used to determine how to record performance marks via the timer service)
The constant value used for AverageBufferSize is 65535 (64KB) when determining whether to split large text content in the piece tree text buffer implementation
Each TreeNode in PieceTreeBase maintains two metadata fields for its left subtree: lf_left (line feed count in left subtree) and size_left (character count/size of left subtree)
The three threshold values for 'too large' files are: LARGE_FILE_SIZE_BOUND (50MB), LARGE_FILE_LINE_COUNT_BOUND (300000 lines), and MODEL_SYNC_LIMIT (50MB for sync threshold)
PieceTreeSearchCache has a cache limit of 1 entry (stores most recent search). When the limit is reached, the old entry is simply replaced with the new one
cursors[0] is the primary cursor (also called the main/principal cursor). The rest of the cursors array contains secondary cursors that follow the primary cursor's behavior
The three possible values of SelectionStartKind enum are: Simple (0), Word (1), and Line (2)
MIN_SAFE_DELTA and MAX_SAFE_DELTA constants are used to ensure safe delta calculations for position tracking. Values are -1073741824 and 1073741824 respectively (2^30 boundaries)
The default ordinal value in ViewZones is 10000 when neither ordinal nor afterColumn is specified for computing zone order
The three ContentWidgetPositionPreference enum values are: EXACT (0), ABOVE (1), and BELOW (2)
EOL is normalized to CRLF if CRLF count > LF count, otherwise normalized to LF. The builder tracks both \r\n and \n occurrences during text processing
The static HEIGHT constant defined for StatusbarPart is 22 pixels
The three possible values for CompositeBarPosition enum in paneCompositePart.ts are: TOP, BOTTOM, and TITLE
ACTION_HEIGHT is 48 pixels. Storage keys for pinned view containers are: 'workbench.activity.pinnedViewlets' and 'workbench.activity.pinnedViewlets2' (for migration)
SidebarPart minimumWidth is 170 pixels. The activeViewletSettingsKey is 'workbench.sidebar.activeviewletid'
PartLayout static height constants are: HEADER_HEIGHT (35), TITLE_HEIGHT (35), and FOOTER_HEIGHT (0 or not defined as constant)
PanelPart minimumHeight is 77 pixels. preferredHeight is calculated based on stored value or defaulting to workbench dimension height * 0.4
LayoutClasses enum CSS class names include: FULLSCREEN, MAXIMIZED, WINDOW_BORDER, and SIDEBAR_HIDDEN (for controlling workbench visibility states)
MIN_COMPOSITE_BAR_WIDTH is 50 pixels. The empty pane message displayed is 'No views are enabled in this view container.' (or similar no views message)
CompositeBar assigns ARIA role='toolbar' and aria-label='Active View Switcher' (or 'Composite Bar') for accessibility
BrowserTitlebarPart minimumHeight returns 35 pixels as the default height when the command center is visible
The two internal properties stored on a constructor function for DI metadata are: '$di$dependencies' (dependency list) and '$di$target' (service identifier/brand)
supportsDelayedInstantiation indicates whether the service can be lazily instantiated via a proxy, deferring actual construction until first method call
The two values in InstantiationType enum are: Eager (0) and Delayed (1)
The Graph class uses findCyclePath() method to detect cyclic dependencies during service instantiation
The three StorageScope values are: APPLICATION (0), PROFILE (1), and WORKSPACE (2)
The four LifecycleMainPhase values in order are: Starting (1), Ready (2), Restored (3), and Eventually (4)
The set() method returns the previous value (SyncDescriptor or instance) that was stored for that service identifier, or undefined if none existed
The default flush interval for AbstractStorageService is 5000 milliseconds (5 seconds)

createDecorator validates that the service ID is a non-empty string. If called with incorrect arguments or if decorator is used as a value, it throws an Error: 'decorator can only be used to decorate a parameter'

BrowserLifecycleService does not support vetos (shutdown cannot be cancelled). Unlike desktop version which can prompt users and handle veto responses, browser version has no veto mechanism due to browser limitations
The default delay for CodeActionOracle auto-triggering is 250 milliseconds
AI-generated code actions are sorted to appear after non-AI code actions. The sorting places AI actions at the bottom of the list
The contribution ID for CodeLensContribution is 'css.codelens' or 'codelens' (registered via EditorContributionRegistry)
The storage key for CodeLensCache is 'codelens/cache'. The old key was 'codelens/cache' (version without v1 prefix)
A Hover result is valid if: 1) contents array is non-empty, and 2) range is either undefined or a valid Range object
MarkdownHoverParticipant hoverOrdinal value is 2 (determining its order among hover participants)
The three SnippetSortOrder enum values are: Top (0), Inline (1), and Bottom (2)
SuggestModel sends 15% (0.15) of completion duration telemetry reports (sampling to reduce telemetry volume)
The configuration key controlling word highlighter delay is 'editor.occurrencesHighlight.delay' (or 'editor.wordHighlighterDelay')
LightBulbWidget registers 6 different icon variations for gutter lightbulb decorations (regular, auto-fix, AI, and their combined variants)
CommandsRegistry uses a Map<string, LinkedList<ICommand>> data structure internally to store commands (allowing multiple handlers per command ID)
PersistedMenuHideState uses storage key 'workbench.menu.hiddenCommands' to persist hidden menu commands
A 'noop' command (or empty/placeholder command) is registered by default in CommandsRegistry at initialization time
When getMenuItems() is called with MenuId.CommandPalette, it returns all registered commands (flattening menu structure) rather than just explicitly registered menu items
When creating a new MenuId with an identifier that already exists, an Error is thrown indicating duplicate MenuId registration
When f1 is true in registerAction2, three things are registered: 1) the command itself, 2) a menu item in CommandPalette (MenuId.CommandPalette), and 3) keybinding if specified
The default eventDebounceDelay value set by MenuService when creating a menu is 50 milliseconds
ActionViewItemService constructs the key using the menu ID and command ID combined: `${menuId.id}/${commandId}` format
The 'navigation' group receives special treatment in _compareMenuItems: it is always sorted first (before all other groups)
The command ID for MenuHiddenStatesReset action is 'workbench.action.resetMenuHiddenStates' (resets all hidden menu states)
